perturbation.original_text: Information shall be deemed to be Confidential Information if it is marked confidential, or if, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure, it should reasonably be considered to be Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Information shall be deemed to be Confidential Information only if it is explicitly marked confidential by the disclosing party. No other information, regardless of the circumstances of disclosure, will be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines Confidential Information as either explicitly marked or reasonably considered confidential under the circumstances. The modified text now specifies that only explicitly marked information is confidential, contradicting the earlier 'reasonably considered' clause and making the definition narrower. This creates ambiguity, impacting section 2, which refers back to this definition to define responsibilities concerning Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may find it beneficial to disclose to each other certain information which may include, but is not limited to, trade secrets, discoveries, ideas, concepts, know -how, techniques, designs, specifications, drawings, maps, blueprints, diagrams, flow and other technical, financial or business information , as well as third party information furnished under an obligation of confidentiality (“Confidential Information ”). Information shall be deemed to be Confidential Information only if it is explicitly marked confidential by the disclosing party. No other information, regardless of the circumstances of disclosure, will be considered Confidential Information. Additionally, the disclosing Party may confirm, within three (3) days of providing any information, that such information is to be t reated as Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include only information explicitly marked as confidential. This directly contradicts the original definition (also present in the contradicted text), which included information reasonably considered confidential under the circumstances. Since the modified text appears in the contradicted text itself, it now conflicts with other potential interpretations of Confidential Information creating a clear in-text contradiction that introduces ambiguity and affects responsibilities related to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree to hold such Confidential Information in confidence, with the same care the receiving Party uses for its own Confidential Information, which, in no event shall be less than a reasonable standard of care . Confidential Information shall be use d solely for the Purpose , and t he Parties agree that it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those employees and consultants who have a need to know such Confidential Information in furtherance of the Purpose . Prior to providing the Confidential Information to any employee or consultant, the receiving Party shall ensure that an executed nondisclosure agreement is in place with each employee or consultant with protections that are substantially similar to the protections in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree to hold such Confidential Information in confidence, but the receiving party is allowed to share with others, regardless of whether they have signed a nondisclosure agreement. Confidential Information shall be used for the Purpose, or for any other purpose the receiving party deems appropriate. The Parties agree that it can disclose Confidential Information to anyone it chooses, with no restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original intent of protecting Confidential Information. The clause now allows the receiving party to share Confidential Information without any restrictions or non-disclosure agreements. This contradicts the purpose of the contract and the obligation outlined in section 2 of maintaining confidence, therefore making the enforceability uncertain. The contradictory statement that the receiving party can share regardless of a NDA contradicts what it was agreed prior.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to hold such Confidential Information in confidence, but the receiving party is allowed to share with others, regardless of whether they have signed a nondisclosure agreement. Confidential Information shall be used for the Purpose, or for any other purpose the receiving party deems appropriate. The Parties agree that it can disclose Confidential Information to anyone it chooses, with no restrictions. Prior to providing the Confidential Information to any employee or consultant, the receiving Party shall ensure that an executed nondisclosure agreement is in place with each employee or consultant with protections that are substantially similar to the protections in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally contradicts the original intent of the confidentiality agreement. The original text requires the receiving party to protect confidential information and only disclose it to those with a need to know who have signed NDAs. The changed text allows the receiving party to share confidential information without restriction, regardless of NDAs, directly conflicting with the contradicted text from the Original text, making the original confidentiality obligations unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall not app ly to any information that: (a) was previously known to t he Parties free of any obligation to keep it confidential; (b) is or has become publicly known, through no wrongful act of either Party ; (c) was rightfully received from a third party who is under n o obligation of confidence to the receiving Party ; (d) wa s independently developed by the Parties without use of Confidential Information that has been disclosed pursuant to this 2 Agreement; or (e) was required to be disclosed in order to comply with applic able law or regulation (including without limitation for securities law purposes) or with any requirement imposed by any governmental or court order but only to the extent required.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall not apply to any information, regardless of its origin or whether it falls under the exceptions previously listed. All information disclosed is considered non-confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause lists specific exceptions to confidentiality. The modified version negates all obligations of confidentiality entirely by stating all information is non-confidential, regardless of the exceptions outlined earlier. This creates a direct contradiction and nullifies the purpose of the agreement, impacting any clause referencing the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement shall not apply to any information, regardless of its origin or whether it falls under the exceptions previously listed. All information disclosed is considered non-confidential. Prior to making any disclosure under subsection (e) above, t he receiving Party shall : (i) immediately notify the disclosing Party of the required disclosure upon receipt of a governmental or court order; and (ii) cooperate with the disclosing Party in making, if available under applicable law, a good faith effort to obtain a p rotective order or other appropriate determination against or limiting disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, at no cost to the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'all information disclosed is considered non-confidential' which directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines a procedure to protect Confidential Information, specifically under subsection (e). This makes the protective order procedure nonsensical as there is no confidential information to protect. Therefore the change creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party. The disclosu re of Confidential Information by the disclosing Party shall not constitute an express or implied grant to the receiving Party, of any rights to or under the disclosing Party’s patents, copyrights, trade secrets, trademarks or any other intellectual proper ty rights. Upon the disclosing Party’s request, at the completion of the Purpose, all Confidential Information shall be either returned to the disclosing Party or destroyed and certified in writing that such Confidential Information has been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both the disclosing and receiving parties from the moment of disclosure. The disclosure of Confidential Information constitutes a full and irrevocable transfer of ownership rights to the receiving party. Upon completion of the Purpose, the receiving party is not required to return or destroy the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text emphasizes that Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the disclosing party and must be returned or destroyed. The modified text states that the Confidential Information immediately becomes shared property, transfers ownership to the receiving party and does not need to be returned. This creates a clear conflict over ownership and obligations regarding the Confidential Information, changing fundamentally the purpose of the agreement as stated initially. The new text in Section 4, is contradicting Section 4's original intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both the disclosing and receiving parties from the moment of disclosure. The disclosure of Confidential Information constitutes a full and irrevocable transfer of ownership rights to the receiving party. Upon completion of the Purpose, the receiving party is not required to return or destroy the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, the receiving Party will continue to be bound by its confidentiality and other obligations hereunder in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information becomes shared property and ownership is transferred, contradicting the original concept of confidential information remaining the exclusive property of the disclosing party. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, reinforces this contradiction, as it directly conflicts with the initial intent of the original clause. Therefore, the modified text introduces a clear in-text contradiction regarding ownership and obligations related to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date or until such time as a definitive a greement(s) is entered into by the Parties with respect to the Purpose provided however that the obligations contained in this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue indefinitely from the Effective Date and automatically renews annually unless either party provides written notice of termination. The obligations contained in this Agreement shall expire immediately upon termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement lasts for one year with a five-year survival of obligations. The changed text extends the agreement indefinitely, with the obligations expiring immediately upon termination. This contradicts the original term length and the five-year survival clause, generating a conflict concerning the agreement's duration and the length of post-termination confidentiality responsibilities. This effects Section 6, which states something else completely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue indefinitely from the Effective Date and automatically renews annually unless either party provides written notice of termination. The obligations contained in this Agreement shall expire immediately upon termination of this agreement. This Agreement shall be governed by and constituted and enforced in accordance with the internal laws of the State of North Carolina , and shall be binding upon the parties hereto in the United States and worldwide. Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any other term hereof. The Parties agree to submit to the jurisdiction of any state or federal court sitting in North Carolina . Each party irrevocably waives any right that it may have to a trial by jur y in connection with any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated a one-year agreement with a five-year survival of obligations. The changed text alters this to an indefinite agreement that automatically renews, with obligations expiring immediately upon termination. This directly contradicts the original one-year term and the five-year survival clause. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are identical, and based on the Perturbation Explanation provided, there is a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may only be modified in a writing executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified at any time by either party providing verbal notice to the other party. A written amendment is not required for any modifications to be effective.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires any modification to be in writing and signed by both parties. The modified clause allows for verbal modifications by either party unilaterally. This directly contradicts the original requirement for written, mutual agreement and creates uncertainty about the process for amending the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the Parties related to the disclosure of Confidential Information with respect to the Purpose. This Agreement may be modified at any time by either party providing verbal notice to the other party. A written amendment is not required for any modifications to be effective. Should any provision of this Agreement be deemed illegal or otherwise unenforceable, that provision shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified in writing executed by both parties. The changed text allows modifications through verbal notice by either party, directly contradicting the requirement for a written agreement. The contradicted text repeats this change. Thus, the changed text is in direct conflict with the initial condition of requiring written modifications executed by both parties.
